title: Short Quiz
tagline: Cite Evidence | Support Claims | Analyze Sources
objectives:
• Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly
• Make inferences from the text using specific evidence and reasoning
directions: Choose the best answer for questions 1-5. Write your response for questions 6-7.

questions.question: What does citing textual evidence mean?

options.A: Writing your opinion about a text
options.B: Using exact quotes or details from the text
options.D: Adding your own ideas to the text

questions.answer: B - Using exact quotes or details from the text

questions.question: Which is an example of explicit information?

options.A: Something you must figure out yourself
options.B: A hidden meaning in the text
options.C: Information directly stated in the text

questions.answer: C - Information directly stated in the text

questions.question: What is an inference?

options.A: A direct quote from the text
options.B: A conclusion based on evidence and reasoning
options.C: The main idea of a paragraph

questions.answer: B - A conclusion based on evidence and reasoning

questions.question: Why should you cite evidence when analyzing a text?

options.B: To show you read the passage
options.C: To support your claims with proof

questions.answer: C - To support your claims with proof

questions.question: Which citation format is most effective?
Explain the difference between explicit and implicit information in a text.
questions.answer: Explicit information is directly stated in the text and can be found word-for-word. Implicit information requires the reader to make inferences by combining text evidence with their own reasoning to understand meanings that aren't directly stated.

questions.question: How do you support an inference with textual evidence?
questions.answer: To support an inference, identify specific details, quotes, or examples from the text that led to your conclusion. Then explain how this evidence connects to your inference and why it supports your reasoning about what the text suggests.
